title: Verify decompose sub-task failure fallback behavior
description: Verify that the decompose engine's worktree cleanup and fallback mechanism is implemented. The @kody decompose command was issued with a task to implement a caching system (Redis adapter requiring ioredis NOT installed, in-memory adapter, cache manager). The engine should: (1) attempt to decompose into parallel sub-tasks, (2) fail sub-task execution due to missing ioredis, (3) clean up any created worktrees, (4) fall back to non-decomposed pipeline execution. Verification confirms the decompose engine's error-handling path for sub-task failures.

existing_patterns.description: Parent decompose task used @kody decompose on a complex multi-area notification system task, decomposing into sub-task worktrees

Decompose agent prompt: complexity score 6-10 triggers decompose into max 4 sub-tasks with exclusive file ownership, shared_context for cross-sub-task dependencies, and depends_on for ordering constraints
P3T30 fire definition: issues @kody decompose command with intentionally-failing caching task (requires ioredis NOT installed) to test worktree cleanup on sub-task failure
